items.headline: US Equities Advance on AI Enthusiasm and Easing Geopolitical Tensions; Nasdaq and S&P 500 Hit Record Highs
items.summary: U.S. stock indices saw gains today, fueled by ongoing investment fervor in Artificial Intelligence and a perceived de-escalation of geopolitical tensions. The Nasdaq Composite and S&P 500 both reached new record highs, extending a bullish trend. Pre-market trading had already indicated a strong start, with futures for the Nasdaq and S&P 500 pointing to record opens despite international conflicts. Overall market sentiment remains optimistic, albeit with some underlying caution regarding inflation and bond market risks.

items.headline: Micron Technology
items.summary: Shares of Micron Technology surged amid robust demand for computer memory, particularly its HBM chips, and a significant price target increase by analysts, solidifying its role in the AI boom.

items.headline: Qualcomm
items.summary: Qualcomm saw significant gains, contributing to the overall strength in the semiconductor and technology sectors today.

items.headline: Advanced Micro Devices
items.summary: Advanced Micro Devices climbed, reflecting the ongoing positive momentum within the AI industrial chain and semiconductor market.

items.headline: AutoZone
items.summary: AutoZone experienced a sharp decline after reporting slightly weaker revenue for its latest quarter than analysts had anticipated.

items.headline: BP plc
items.summary: BP's stock fell on the London exchange following the ousting of its chairman due to concerns related to governance standards, oversight, and conduct.

items.headline: IBM
items.summary: IBM was among the declining components within the Dow Jones Industrial Average today.

items.headline: Sector Performance: Tech and Healthcare Lead, Communication Services Lag
items.summary: The AI industrial chain, particularly semiconductors, emerged as a clear leader today, driving significant market movement. Technology, industrial, and healthcare sectors generally led gains as investors rotated back into risk assets. Conversely, the Communication Services sector lagged, experiencing a decline. Healthcare was noted as the only sector to deliver negative earnings growth for the first quarter.

items.headline: New Fed Chair Sworn In; Rate Hike Bets Rise
items.summary: Kevin Warsh was officially sworn in as the new Chairman of the Federal Reserve on Friday. His first Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meeting is scheduled for June 16-17. The bond market is currently pricing in a 70% chance of a Fed rate hike by the end of the year, with the highest odds for a single quarter-point increase from the current 3.50%-3.75% target.

items.headline: Consumer Confidence Dips; Key Inflation Data Expected Later This Week
items.summary: U.S. consumer confidence edged downwards in May, though the decline was not as severe as economists had feared, following a report on Friday that indicated consumer sentiment hitting a record low. Looking ahead, investors are anticipating the release of Personal Consumption Expenditures (PCE) inflation data and the second revision of first-quarter GDP figures on Wednesday. Additional data on new home sales and building permits is expected on Thursday.

items.headline: Geopolitical Tensions with Iran Ease, Influencing Oil and Yields
items.summary: Hopes for a diplomatic resolution to the conflict with Iran appear to be easing geopolitical tensions, which in turn has influenced oil prices and Treasury yields. This perceived de-escalation contributed to today's positive market sentiment, with oil prices generally moving lower over the weekend.

items.headline: Q1 Earnings Strong, AI Continues to Drive Tech Sector
items.summary: The first-quarter earnings season is winding down, with major companies like Dell ($DELL), Marvell Technology ($MRVL), and Salesforce ($CRM) scheduled to report this week. Q1 earnings have been robust, particularly for S&P 500 blue-chips and AI-related firms. Nvidia notably delivered strong results, with its CEO stating that 'demand has gone parabolic.' The AI investment craze remains a dominant structural rally, particularly benefiting the semiconductor industry.

items.headline: Goldman Sachs Warns Bond Market is Key Risk to Equities
items.summary: Goldman Sachs has issued a warning that the bond market now poses the biggest risk to equities. This concern is attributed to an over-concentration in technology stocks and prevailing geopolitical malaise. Not financial advice.
